---
title: "Code Audit"
author: "Tianqi Ouyang"
date: last-modified
---

Systematic review of `Tables.Rmd`, `revision 0311.Rmd`, and `new_reqeust_0311.Rmd` for bugs, logic errors, and inconsistencies.

## Critical Bugs {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-tip}
## Bugs 1, 3, and 9 have been FIXED (2026-04-01).
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Remaining issues 14, 17, and 18 affect survival analysis and Fine-Gray models.
:::

## 1. INU patients assigned MCM ID prefix {#bug-1}

**Severity:** Critical -- data corruption
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 63, `revision 0311.Rmd` line 63

```r
# Current (WRONG):
inu <- read_csv('...INU/...') %>% ... %>% mutate(id = paste0("mcm_", row_number()))

# Should be:
inu <- read_csv('...INU/...') %>% ... %>% mutate(id = paste0("inu_", row_number()))
```

The INU dataset is assigned IDs with prefix `"mcm_"` instead of `"inu_"`. MCM (line 61) also uses `"mcm_"`, so INU and MCM patients share `id` values. Any downstream operation relying on `id` as a unique identifier will have collisions.

## 2. `scr_terli_day7` missing from `scr_vars` list {#bug-2}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 381--389, `revision 0311.Rmd` same lines

```r
scr_vars <- c(
  ..., "scr_terli_day6", "scr_terli_day8", ...
  # scr_terli_day7 is skipped
)
```

Day 7 creatinine is omitted from the `scr_vars` vector. If this list is used for selection or iteration, day 7 data is silently excluded.

## 3. Child-Pugh score uses telemetry instead of bilirubin {#bug-3}

**Severity:** Critical -- wrong clinical score for all patients
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 881, `revision 0311.Rmd` line 1040

```r
# Current (WRONG):
child_pugh_score = mapply(child_pugh_score,
  tele_terli_day0,     # <-- This is telemetry (yesno), NOT bilirubin!
  alb_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0, ascites_aki, he_terli_day0)

# Should be:
child_pugh_score = mapply(child_pugh_score,
  bili_terli_day0,     # Total bilirubin
  alb_terli_day0, inr_terli_day0, ascites_aki, he_terli_day0)
```

The `child_pugh_score()` function's first argument is `tbili` (total bilirubin), but it receives `tele_terli_day0` (continuous telemetry, a binary 0/1 variable). Every patient's Child-Pugh score is computed with a bilirubin value of 0 or 1, giving a bilirubin sub-score of 1 for all patients.

## 4. `estimate_creatinine` not vectorized {#bug-4}

**Severity:** Low (latent)
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 183--207

The function uses scalar `if/else` and `optimize()`. It works only on one patient at a time. Currently called via `mapply()` which is correct, but the function silently uses only the first element of `sex` if a vector is ever passed directly.

## 5. CLIF-C score: NA components scored as 0 {#bug-5}

**Severity:** Moderate -- biases scores downward for missing data
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 211--275

```r
clif_score <- clif_score + case_when(
  is.na(tb_admit) ~ 0,   # Missing bilirubin = score 0 (should be NA)
  tb_admit < 6 ~ 1,
  ...
)
```

When a lab value is NA, the sub-score is 0 instead of propagating NA. Patients with missing data get artificially lower (better) CLIF-C scores. A patient missing all labs would have an organ score of 0, which is clinically meaningless.

## 6. CLIF-C respiratory score inconsistently handles NA {#bug-6}

**Severity:** Low
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 261--272

When `fio2_admission` is NA, the respiratory sub-score is simply not added (stays 0), while other NA components explicitly get 0 via `case_when`. The final formula `10 * (0.33*score + 0.04*age + 0.63*log(wbc) - 2)` will produce NA if `age` or `wbc` is NA, creating inconsistent missing behavior.

## 7. `HRS_criteria_meld` bare variable comparison {#bug-7}

**Severity:** Low
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 977

```r
# Current:
HRS_criteria_meld = if_else((criteria_2==1 | criteria_3==1 | criteria_4==1 | criteria_5), 1, 0)

# Should be:
HRS_criteria_meld = if_else((criteria_2==1 | criteria_3==1 | criteria_4==1 | criteria_5==1), 1, 0)
```

`criteria_5` is used as a bare value (not `== 1`). Works by coercion when 0/1, but produces NA if `criteria_5` is NA.

## 8. `last_scr_2` errors when `Discont_Day == 0` {#bug-8}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 815, `revision 0311.Rmd` line 900

```r
last_scr_2 = get(paste0("scr_terli_day", Discont_Day - 1))
# When Discont_Day = 0, this tries to get "scr_terli_day-1" → ERROR
```

Patients discontinued on day 0 will cause `get("scr_terli_day-1")` which does not exist. Add an `ifelse(Discont_Day > 0, ..., NA)` guard.

## 9. `subgroup_master` is always empty {#bug-9}

**Severity:** Critical -- subgroup analysis produces empty dataset
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 703--704, `revision 0311.Rmd` same

```r
final_master <- final_master %>%
  filter(!subjectid %in% removed_id, !subjectid %in% subgroup_id)  # removes subgroup
subgroup_master <- final_master %>%
  filter(subjectid %in% subgroup_id)  # tries to find them → 0 rows
```

`subgroup_id` subjects were already removed from `final_master`. The second filter always returns zero rows. Fix: create `subgroup_master` **before** filtering `final_master`.

## 10. `mean()` inside `rowwise()` computes single-row mean {#bug-10}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 871

```r
# Inside rowwise() context:
bili_cal = coalesce(bili_terli_day0, tbili_admit, mean(bili_terli_day0, na.rm = T))
```

In `rowwise()` mode, `mean(bili_terli_day0)` operates on a single value (the current row's value, which is already NA if we reached this fallback). `mean(NA, na.rm=T)` returns `NaN`, not the intended column mean. Compute the column mean before the `rowwise()` pipe.

## 11. Trailing space in variable name {#bug-11}

**Severity:** Low
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 1323

```r
"mido_maxdose_before_terli "  # trailing space
```

Will not match column name `mido_maxdose_before_terli`. Will cause `CreateTableOne` to silently drop this variable or error.

## 12. Wrong variable list in hospitalization course table {#bug-12}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line ~1990

```r
# Copy-paste error: uses HRS clinical course variables instead of hospitalization course
num_hospitalization_course_variables_formula <- as.formula(
  paste("~", paste(num_hrs_clinical_course_variables, collapse = "+"), "|HRS_reversal_new")
)
```

The table for hospitalization course shows HRS clinical course variables instead.

## 13. CLIF-C HE grade 0 scores as 0 instead of 1 {#bug-13}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 234--240

```r
he_grade_admission == 1 ~ 1,
he_grade_admission %in% c(2, 3) ~ 2,
he_grade_admission %in% c(4, 5) ~ 3,
TRUE ~ 0    # HE grade 0 (no encephalopathy) falls here → score 0
```

Per CLIF-SOFA, HE grade 0 (no encephalopathy) should score 1, not 0. All patients without HE get an artificially lower brain sub-score.

## 14. `dc_days_to_death` overwritten for survivors {#bug-14}

**Severity:** High
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 790, `revision 0311.Rmd` line 790

```r
dc_days_to_death = if_else(dc_death_90days == 2 | dc_death_90days == 0, 90, dc_days_to_death)
```

For alive patients (`dc_death_90days == 0`), `dc_days_to_death` is set to 90. Then `time_to_death` for non-lost-to-followup survivors becomes `days_to_discharge + 90` regardless of actual last encounter time. Should use `dc_days_lastencounter` for survivors.

## new_reqeust_0311.Rmd Issues

## 15. Outcome variables imputed alongside predictors {#bug-15}

**Severity:** Methodological concern
**File:** `new_reqeust_0311.Rmd` lines 93--123

`mi_data` includes `HRS_reversal_new`, `hrs_responders_cat_2`, `time_to_death_90days_cmp`, and `time_to_death_status_90days_cmp` in the imputation model with `method = "pmm"` applied to all. Imputing outcome values via PMM can bias effect estimates. Standard practice: include outcomes as predictors in the imputation model but do not impute their missing values.

## 16. `discont_before_day3` does not handle NA {#bug-16}

**Severity:** Low
**File:** `new_reqeust_0311.Rmd` line 39

```r
discont_before_day3 = ifelse(Discont_Day < 3, 1, 0)
# NA Discont_Day → NA (unlike pc_change_before_day3 which gets NA→0 fix)
```

## 17. Fine-Gray pooling lacks Rubin's rules {#bug-17}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `new_reqeust_0311.Rmd` lines 200--204

```r
coef_mat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(fit_crr_list, function(f) f$coef))
coef_mean <- colMeans(coef_mat)
hr_est <- exp(coef_mean)  # No SE, CI, or p-values
```

Simple coefficient averaging without variance pooling. The point estimates may be reasonable but no standard errors, confidence intervals, or p-values are produced.

Note: `revision 0311.Rmd` has a proper `run_pooled_fg()` function that **does** implement Rubin's rules correctly (within-imputation variance + between-imputation variance pooling). The issue is only in `new_reqeust_0311.Rmd`.

## 18. Fine-Gray competing risk: `time_to_death_status_90days_cmp` encoding depends on bug #14 {#bug-18}

**Severity:** High -- affects all Fine-Gray models
**File:** All files using `Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp)`

The competing risk status variable `time_to_death_status_90days_cmp` is derived from:

```r
time_to_death_status_90days_cmp = case_when(
  time_to_death_90days <= time_to_transplant_90days ~ time_to_death_status_90days,
  time_to_death_90days >  time_to_transplant_90days ~ time_to_transplant_status_90days)
```

This has **two related issues**:

1. **Depends on bug #14 (survivor time = 90):** Since survivors have `dc_days_to_death` set to 90 (line 790), their `time_to_death_90days` may equal `time_to_transplant_90days` = 90 (both capped). When times are tied, the `<=` in the first `case_when` branch assigns the death status, but these patients did NOT die -- they just have artificial time = 90. This means some alive non-transplanted patients are handled via the death-status branch (which correctly gives 0 = censored in this case, so the **status** is okay, but the **time** is inflated).

2. **Tie-breaking direction when death time = transplant time:** When `time_to_death_90days == time_to_transplant_90days`, the current code assigns death status over transplant status. If a patient died AND was transplanted at the same time point (e.g., both capped at 90), death takes precedence. This is a modeling choice, not necessarily a bug, but should be documented.

**The real issue** is that `time_to_death_90days` for survivors is wrong (bug #14), which inflates censoring times for alive patients, potentially biasing cumulative incidence estimates downward.

## 19. `time_to_death_status_90days_cmp` converted to factor inconsistently {#bug-19}

**Severity:** Low
**File:** `new_reqeust_0311.Rmd` line 177

```r
dat$time_to_death_status_90days_cmp <- as.factor(dat$time_to_death_status_90days_cmp)
```

`tidycmprsk::crr()` requires the status variable to be a factor for competing risk specification. This conversion is done **inside** the `lapply()` loop, which is correct. However, the factor levels depend on which values are present in each imputed dataset. If one imputation has no transplant events (status=2), the factor will have only levels 0 and 1, changing the interpretation. This is unlikely but possible in small subgroup analyses.

**Recommendation:** Explicitly set factor levels: `factor(dat$status, levels = c(0, 1, 2))`.

---

# Clinical Definition Audit

## 20. MELD 3.0: sodium not capped at 125--137 {#bug-20}

**Severity:** High
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 923--933, 943--951

```r
# Current (WRONG):
0.82 * (137 - na_terli_day0) - 0.24 * (137 - na_terli_day0) * log(bili_terli_day0)

# Should be (per Kim et al., Gastroenterology 2023):
0.82 * (137 - pmin(pmax(na_terli_day0, 125), 137)) - ...
```

The published MELD 3.0 formula caps sodium at 125--137 (same as MELD-Na). Uncapped sodium means hyponatremic patients get inflated scores and hypernatremic patients get paradoxically lower scores.

## 21. MELD 3.0: albumin not capped at 1.5--3.5 {#bug-21}

**Severity:** High
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 923--933, 943--951

```r
# Current (WRONG):
1.85 * (3.5 - alb_terli_day0) - 1.83 * (3.5 - alb_terli_day0) * log(creatinine_adj_day0)

# Should use capped albumin:
alb_capped = pmin(pmax(alb_terli_day0, 1.5), 3.5)
1.85 * (3.5 - alb_capped) - 1.83 * (3.5 - alb_capped) * log(creatinine_adj_day0)
```

MELD 3.0 caps albumin at 1.5--3.5 g/dL. Patients with albumin > 3.5 get a negative albumin contribution, reducing the score.

## 22. MELD formulas: bilirubin and INR not floored at 1.0 {#bug-22}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 919, 928--929, 939, 948--949

Per UNOS specification, bilirubin, INR, and creatinine should all be floored at 1.0 (set to 1 if < 1). Creatinine is correctly floored (`creatinine_adj_day0`), but bilirubin and INR are not. `log(x)` for x < 1 is negative, reducing the score. Rare in HRS but technically incorrect.

## 23. CLIF-SOFA kidney failure: threshold should be 2.0, not 1.5 {#bug-23}

**Severity:** High
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 315, 963

```r
# Current (WRONG):
kidney_f = ifelse(!is.na(scr_terli_day0) & scr_terli_day0 >= 1.5, 1, 0)

# Per CLIF-SOFA (Moreau et al., Gastroenterology 2013):
# Kidney failure (CLIF-SOFA = 3) is SCr >= 2.0
# SCr 1.5-1.9 is kidney dysfunction (CLIF-SOFA = 2), NOT failure
kidney_f = ifelse(!is.na(scr_terli_day0) & scr_terli_day0 >= 2.0, 1, 0)
```

Using &ge; 1.5 overcounts kidney failure, inflating ACLF grades. Directly affects `criteria_2` (ACLF &ge; 3) and all futility criteria analyses. Note: since all patients have HRS-AKI, most will have SCr &ge; 2.0 anyway, but the threshold matters for borderline cases.

## 24. CLIF-SOFA circulatory failure: ignores vasopressor requirement {#bug-24}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 305, 957

```r
# Current:
circulatory_f = ifelse(!is.na(map_terli_day0_time0) & map_terli_day0_time0 < 70, 1, 0)

# Per CLIF-SOFA: circulatory failure (score=3) = use of vasopressors
# MAP < 70 without vasopressors = score 2 (dysfunction, not failure)
```

The `calculate_clif_c_score` function correctly uses MAP + vasopressor logic for sub-scoring, but the `organ_failure_sum` counts MAP < 70 alone as "failure." Inconsistent between the two functions.

## 25. AKIN Stage 1: delta uses `> 0.3` instead of `>= 0.3` {#bug-25}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` line 865

```r
# Current (WRONG):
akin = case_when(
  (ratio >= 1.5 & ratio < 2) | (ratio < 1.5 & delta > 0.3) ~ 1, ...)
                                                    # ^ should be >=

# Correctly done in discont_aki_stage:
  (discont_ratio < 1.5 & discont_delta >= 0.3) ~ 1  # uses >=
```

AKIN/KDIGO Stage 1 = increase &ge; 0.3 mg/dL. The initial staging uses `>` while discontinuation staging correctly uses `>=`. Patients with delta exactly 0.3 are misclassified at initiation.

## 27. `baseline_scr_new`: NA in `baseline_scr_days_to_admit` causes fallthrough {#bug-27}

**Severity:** Moderate
**File:** `Tables.Rmd` lines 856--858

```r
baseline_scr_new = case_when(
  baseline_scr_days_to_admit != 0 & !is.na(baseline_scr) ~ baseline_scr,  # NA != 0 → NA → skip
  baseline_scr_days_to_admit == 0 & ... ~ ...,                             # NA == 0 → NA → skip
  baseline_scr_days_to_admit == 0 & ... ~ ...)                             # NA == 0 → NA → skip
```

If `baseline_scr_days_to_admit` is NA, all three branches evaluate to NA, and `baseline_scr_new` is NA even when `baseline_scr` has a valid value. Silently drops patients from AKIN staging.

---

# Design Decisions to Document

## 29. `criteria_4`: missing FiO2/SpO2 treated as no respiratory failure {#note-29}

**Severity:** Low (design decision)

Missing SpO2/FiO2 &rarr; NA ratio &rarr; `criteria_4 = NA` &rarr; recoded to 0. Assumes missing = no respiratory compromise.

## 30. `race_2`: missing race grouped with White {#note-30}

**Severity:** Moderate (equity concern)

`race_2 = if_else(race == 1 | is.na(race), 1, 0)` -- patients with missing race data are classified as White/Caucasian. Could bias adjusted analyses. Consider a separate "unknown" category.

## 31. Median imputation for `admit_meld_3` and `clif_c_score_new` {#note-31}

**Severity:** Moderate (design decision)

Single-value imputation underestimates variance. These imputed versions are used in criteria flags and potentially in models. The multiple imputation in `new_reqeust_0311.Rmd` does this more correctly.

## 32. `total_albumin`: `rowSums(na.rm=TRUE)` treats missing days as 0 dose {#note-32}

**Severity:** Moderate (design decision)

A patient with 7 days of data and 7 missing days gets the same `total_albumin` as a patient who genuinely received 0g on those 7 days. Systematically underestimates albumin exposure for patients with incomplete data.

## 34. `calculate_clif_c_score` parameter named `peak_creatinine` but receives `scr_terli_day0` {#note-34}

**Severity:** Low (naming)

Misleading parameter name. The function is called with day 0 terlipressin creatinine, not peak hospitalization creatinine.

---

## Summary Table

::: {.callout-note}
Bugs 1, 3, 9 are now **FIXED**.
:::

| # | Severity | Category | Issue | Status |
|--:|:---------|:---------|:------|:-------|
| 1 | **Critical** | Code | INU patients get MCM IDs | **FIXED** |
| 3 | **Critical** | Code | Child-Pugh uses telemetry instead of bilirubin | **FIXED** |
| 9 | **Critical** | Code | subgroup_master always empty | **FIXED** |
| 20 | **High** | Clinical | MELD 3.0 sodium not capped 125--137 | Open |
| 21 | **High** | Clinical | MELD 3.0 albumin not capped 1.5--3.5 | Open |
| 23 | **High** | Clinical | CLIF-SOFA kidney failure threshold 1.5 should be 2.0 | Open |
| 14 | **High** | Code | Survivor time-to-death uses 90 instead of actual follow-up | Open |
| 18 | **High** | Code | Fine-Gray competing risk times inflated (from #14) | Open |
| 25 | Moderate | Clinical | AKIN delta `> 0.3` should be `>= 0.3` (inconsistent) | Open |
| 24 | Moderate | Clinical | Circulatory failure ignores vasopressor requirement | Open |
| 22 | Moderate | Clinical | Bilirubin/INR not floored at 1.0 in MELD | Open |
| 27 | Moderate | Code | NA in baseline_scr_days_to_admit causes fallthrough | Open |
| 5 | Moderate | Code | CLIF-C treats missing labs as score 0 | Open |
| 8 | Moderate | Code | last_scr_2 errors when Discont_Day=0 | Open |
| 10 | Moderate | Code | mean() inside rowwise() is single-value | Open |
| 12 | Moderate | Code | Wrong variable list in hospitalization table | Open |
| 13 | Moderate | Code | CLIF-C HE grade 0 scored as 0 not 1 | Open |
| 15 | Moderate | Code | Outcomes imputed alongside predictors | Open |
| 17 | Moderate | Code | Fine-Gray pooling lacks Rubin's rules | Open |
| 2 | Moderate | Code | scr_terli_day7 missing from scr_vars | Open |
| 30 | Moderate | Design | Missing race grouped with White | Open |
| 31 | Moderate | Design | Median imputation for MELD 3.0 / CLIF-C | Open |
| 32 | Moderate | Design | total_albumin treats missing days as 0 dose | Open |
| 19 | Low | Code | Factor levels not explicit in Fine-Gray | Open |
| 7 | Low | Code | HRS_criteria_meld bare variable | Open |
| 11 | Low | Code | Trailing space in variable name | Open |
| 4 | Low | Code | estimate_creatinine not vectorized | Open |
| 6 | Low | Code | CLIF-C respiratory NA handling | Open |
| 16 | Low | Code | discont_before_day3 NA handling | Open |
| 29 | Low | Design | Missing FiO2/SpO2 = no respiratory failure | Open |
| 34 | Low | Design | CLIF-C parameter named peak_creatinine | Open |
